finding: Tesla estimated a propagation time (0.08484 s) that converts to ~11.78 Hz, but never derived the disc-resonance formula f=c/(2D) attributed to him. The formula requires D=12,717 km — Earth's diameter, not any dome parameter
detail_claim: US Patent 787412 contains formula f = c/(2*disc_thickness) giving 11.787 Hz.
detail_evidence:
US Patent 787412 (1905), publicly available at https://patents.google.com/patent/US787412A, describes electrical energy transmission through the Earth. Tesla estimated that signals travel 'to and returning from the region diametrically opposite the pole over the earth's surface with a mean velocity of about four hundred and seventy-one thousand two hundred and forty kilometers per second,' yielding a round-trip time he calculated as 'not less than one-twelfth or probably 0.08484 of a second.' Taking the reciprocal gives ~11.79 Hz — so the frequency value genuinely traces to Tesla. However, the specific equation f = c/(2D) framed as a disc-thickness resonance formula does not appear in the patent. Tesla was describing surface wave propagation around a spherical Earth at ~1.57c (a superluminal phase velocity characteristic of the Earth-ionosphere waveguide), not a flat-disc standing-wave resonance.

The dome's formula exposes the relabeling. Solving f = c/(2D) = 11.787 Hz gives D = 12,717 km. This matches Earth's diameter (12,742 km) to 0.2% — but corresponds to no parameter in the dome model. The dome's disc radius is 20,015 km, firmament apex height is 9,086 km, and disc diameter is 40,030 km. The number 12,717 km is the globe's diameter and nothing else. By calling it 'disc_thickness,' the dome model smuggles in a globe parameter under a new name. Furthermore, 11.78 Hz is distinct from the Schumann resonance (7.83 Hz); conflating them misidentifies two separate electromagnetic phenomena.
detail_verdict_text: The 11.78 Hz value traces to Tesla's estimate, but the dome model misrepresents spherical-Earth surface wave propagation as a flat-disc resonance formula that Tesla never derived. The formula itself requires Earth's diameter (12,717 km) — not any dome structural parameter.
detail_extra: WIN-001 claims a fundamental resonance of 11.78 Hz while WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (with 26% aetheric damping reducing from 10.6 Hz). These are incompatible: if the disc thickness resonance is 11.78 Hz, the Schumann frequency should be near that value, not 7.83 Hz. The model claims credit for two different frequencies from two incompatible calculations.

finding: Dome cavity with H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz. The 10.59 Hz 'raw theoretical' is Schumann's 1952 globe formula. WIN-029's Schumann-derived height (9,572 km) exceeds the dome's own ceiling (8,537 km) by 12%
detail_claim: Gap between theoretical 10.59 Hz and measured 7.83 Hz proves aetheric damping.
detail_evidence:
The author uses f = c/(4H₀) with a uniform cavity height. But his own model states H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km — an exponentially decaying dome, not a uniform slab. The linear radial average gives H_eff ≈ 3,300 km → f = c/(4 × 3,300) = 22.6 Hz; the area-weighted average gives H_eff ≈ 2,100 km → 35 Hz. Both are far from the observed 7.83 Hz. Any reasonable spatial averaging produces frequencies far above 7.83 Hz; the exact mode structure would require numerical solution of Maxwell's equations in the dome geometry, but no such solution exists in the model's codebase. The author avoids this by pretending the dome has uniform height — but his V12 'key discovery' was specifically that it doesn't. You cannot claim H(r) is exponential for coordinate fits and uniform for Schumann.

The 10.59 Hz is a globe formula. The 'raw theoretical' 10.59 Hz is Schumann's 1952 derivation for a spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity: f₁ = (c/2πa)√2 where a = 6,371 km is Earth's radius. The dome borrows the globe's prediction, observes it doesn't match reality, and attributes the 26% gap to 'aetheric damping.' This is not a dome prediction — it is a globe prediction with a dome-flavored reinterpretation of the residual.

The dome's own heights contradict each other. WIN-029 inverts the quarter-wave formula to derive H = c/(4 × 7.83) = 9,572 km as the 'Schumann cavity height.' But the dome's own H(r) has a maximum of 8,537 km at r=0 (the pole) and decreases monotonically outward. The function can never reach 9,572 km at any point on the disc — the Schumann-derived height exceeds the dome's own ceiling by 1,035 km (12.1%).

The code confirms the vacuity. Domain #1 in monitor.py hardcodes predicted=7.83, observed=7.83, error=0.0%. No formula is evaluated, no data is fetched. The Schumann 'prediction' is a tautological test. Meanwhile, standard ionospheric physics (Sentman, 1995) reproduces 7.83 Hz and all five Schumann harmonics from measured ionospheric conductivity profiles. See Section 4.5.1 for the full derivation.
detail_verdict_text: The 10.59 Hz is Schumann's 1952 globe formula, not a dome derivation. The dome's own geometry gives 22–35 Hz. The Schumann-derived height (9,572 km) exceeds the dome's own ceiling (8,537 km). The monitoring code hardcodes the 'prediction' to equal the observation.
detail_extra: If we compute actual electromagnetic resonant modes for the dome's flat-disc geometry (cylindrical cavity, radius 20,015 km, conductive ceiling), the fundamental TM₀₁ mode is f = c·j₀₁/(2πR) = 5.73 Hz, where j₀₁ = 2.4048 is the first zero of the Bessel function J₀. The TM₁₁ mode gives 9.13 Hz. Neither matches 7.83 Hz, and neither uses the dome's quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H). The quarter-wave formula describes vertical standing waves between ground and ceiling — a fundamentally different mode from the circumferential Schumann resonance. The dome model conflates two distinct physical phenomena.

finding: 117 Hz is acoustic resonance of a granite sarcophagus, not electromagnetic dome resonance. The '10th harmonic' claim is undefined: 10×7.83 Hz = 78.3 Hz (not 117). No mechanism connects stone acoustics to EM fields
detail_claim: The Great Pyramid's King's Chamber resonates at 117 Hz, matching the 10th harmonic of the dome model.
detail_evidence:
117 Hz is the acoustic resonance frequency of the King's Chamber sarcophagus, documented by John Stuart Reid's cymatics experiments (1996–1997). This is an acoustic eigenmode determined by the sarcophagus geometry and the speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s) — standard Helmholtz resonance with no electromagnetic component. Acoustic resonance and electromagnetic resonance operate on entirely different physical principles: stone vibrates due to mechanical energy; EM cavities resonate due to electromagnetic wave reflection. The dome model provides zero mechanism for electromagnetic dome fields to drive acoustic vibrations in stone.

The harmonic claim is numerically undefined. The dome claims 117 Hz is the '10th harmonic of dome resonance,' but never specifies the base frequency. If 10 × 7.83 Hz (Schumann, WIN-002) = 78.3 Hz — off by a factor of 1.49. If 10 × 11.7 Hz (Tesla, WIN-001) = 117 Hz — a numerical coincidence with no physical mechanism connecting dome EM resonance to stone acoustics. The claim is not a prediction but a post-hoc pattern match.

The code confirms no calculation. WIN-003 has no domain test in monitor.py. It exists only as hardcoded metadata in inject_ai_layer.py: {id: WIN-003, observed: 117.0 Hz, status: confirmed}. No formula is evaluated, no harmonic relationship is computed, no acoustic-to-electromagnetic coupling is modeled.
detail_verdict_text: 117 Hz is acoustic sarcophagus resonance (Helmholtz eigenmode), not electromagnetic. The '10th harmonic' claim fails numerically for Schumann (78.3 Hz ≠ 117 Hz) and offers no physical mechanism for the Tesla coincidence (117 Hz = 10 × 11.7 Hz). The code never computes this claim.
detail_extra: Note on sources: Reid's acoustic measurements of the King's Chamber sarcophagus are documented in his cymatics research and conference presentations, not in peer-reviewed acoustics journals. The dome model cites 'Reid 1997' without a verifiable academic reference. The underlying acoustic measurement is real; the attribution to dome cosmology is unfounded.

finding: MHD core dynamics derive SAA splitting from first principles; dome has no fluid mechanism. Author concedes method invalid
detail_claim: Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay.
detail_evidence: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrate that reversed-flux patches in MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting — including the timing, geometry, and rate of separation. The dome model has no MHD equations, no core dynamics, and no mechanism for generating reversed-flux patches. It cannot explain why the SAA splits, only that it does. Note: V51.0 now acknowledges WIN-004's 'station ratio proxy method' was 'methodologically invalid.'
detail_verdict_text: MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting from core dynamics. The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism for flux-patch separation. Author concedes methodology was invalid.

finding: MHD core dynamics explain differential decay; dome has no fluid mechanism for asymmetric rates
detail_claim: African SAA cell decays faster than Atlantic cell.
detail_evidence: Outer-core convection dynamics in MHD simulations reproduce this asymmetry. Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrate that reversed-flux patches in the core generate the SAA splitting and differential decay rates matching observations. The westward drift of the Atlantic cell is well-documented by NOAA's continuous monitoring.
detail_verdict_text: The differential decay rate arises from reversed-flux patches in the outer core — a fluid-dynamics process that requires a spherical conducting shell. The dome model has no fluid core, no MHD equations, and no mechanism to produce asymmetric decay rates between two SAA cells.

finding: Non-discriminating: linear drift predicted by both models; dome offers no distinct drift mechanism
detail_claim: North Pole drift from 1600–1990 follows a linear trend.
detail_evidence: Magnetic pole position records from NOAA (1600–present) document the North Pole's westward drift at roughly 9 km per year before 1990. The key question: does this drift distinguish the dome from the globe? It does not. Both models can fit a linear trend to the same NOAA data. On the globe, the drift is driven by core-mantle boundary convection patterns (spherical harmonic models). The dome model invokes 'aetheric vortex dynamics' but provides no equations, no predicted drift rate, and no mechanism that would produce a different drift from what is observed. A genuine prediction would specify the drift rate before seeing the data — the dome model has never done this for any magnetic pole trajectory.
detail_verdict_text: Linear drift is non-discriminating: the dome's 'aetheric vortex' has no mechanism that produces a distinct drift rate or pattern from core-mantle convection. Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well.

finding: Standard geophysics explains the 1990s acceleration via flux lobe dynamics; dome provides only a label with no derivation or predictive content
detail_claim: North Pole drift accelerated sharply around 1990, marking a phase transition in pole movement.
detail_evidence:
The NMP acceleration around 1990 is real and well-documented. NOAA NP.xy data shows drift rates rising from ~15 km/yr in 1989 to ~60 km/yr by 2002 (Chulliat & Newitt, 2010, EOS). This acceleration was concentrated in the 1990s and is scientifically significant. However, standard geophysics provides the complete causal mechanism: Livermore et al. (2020, Nature Geoscience) show that two competing magnetic flux lobes on the core-mantle boundary drive the pole's motion, with elongation of the Canadian negative flux lobe between 1970–1999 weakening its pull and allowing the Siberian lobe to dominate.

Neither model predicted this in advance. The 1990s acceleration was documented observationally by Newitt et al. (2002) and analyzed by Mandea & Dormy (2003) before the dome model existed. The dome's 'prediction' was made post hoc.

'Phase transition' is a category error. Calling the acceleration a 'thermodynamic phase transition analogous to 2nd-order transitions' requires an order parameter, symmetry breaking, and critical scaling exponents. Core flow is a magnetohydrodynamic system, not a thermodynamic one undergoing equilibrium phase change. The dome imports impressive terminology without the underlying physics.

The dome's code validates nothing about the acceleration. Domain #11 in monitor.py fetches NOAA NP.xy data but only checks whether the current longitude exceeds 130°E — a trivially true test since the pole is at ~139°E. No code tests for a phase transition, computes acceleration rates, or validates the '1990' timing. The 'prediction' is a post-hoc label on a known observation, confirmed by a longitude check that any model would pass.
detail_verdict_text: The NMP acceleration is real but fully explained by standard flux lobe dynamics (Livermore 2020). The dome provides no mechanism, no derivation, and no advance prediction — only a post-hoc label ('aetheric threshold crossing') for a well-documented observation.

finding: 11-12 Hz is a Schumann inter-harmonic minimum, not a dome resonance peak; first harmonic at 14.3 Hz disproves any 'cutoff'
detail_claim: Sharp telluric cutoff at 11.7 Hz and peak at ~12 Hz match disc resonance ceiling.
detail_evidence:
The 11–12 Hz region falls between the Schumann fundamental (~7.83 Hz) and the first Schumann harmonic (~14.3 Hz), where the natural electromagnetic spectrum has a local power minimum — a standard feature of the spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance spectrum (Chave & Jones, 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method). A 'sharp cutoff' at 11.7 Hz is directly falsified by the robust first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz, which is routinely measured at monitoring stations worldwide. If there were a genuine resonance ceiling at 11.7 Hz, no electromagnetic energy could sustain cavity modes above that frequency — yet harmonics at 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, and 33.8 Hz all exist.

The inter-harmonic minimum the dome identifies is itself a prediction of standard spherical-cavity physics — it is another instance of relabeling globe predictions as dome evidence. The dome's own code labels this a (WIN-008 static win): Domain #25 in monitor.py hardcodes both predicted and observed values as 11.7 Hz with 0.0% error and pass=True. No MT data is fetched from any source.
detail_verdict_text: The first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz directly falsifies any 'cutoff' at 11.7 Hz. The inter-harmonic power minimum is a standard feature of spherical-cavity resonance physics.

finding: Working geophysicists deliberately avoid this noisy frequency band
detail_claim: Telluric frequency spectrum shows a prominent ~12 Hz peak.
detail_evidence: The ~12 Hz region has lower spectral power density than the Schumann peaks because it lies between cavity harmonics (~7.83 Hz fundamental, ~14.3 Hz first harmonic). This is not a 'prominent peak' — it is an inter-harmonic power minimum predicted by standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics (Chave & Jones, 2012). The robust first harmonic at ~14.3 Hz directly contradicts any resonance ceiling below that frequency. See WIN-008 for full analysis.
detail_verdict_text: The ~12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic power minimum, not a peak. The first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz proves no resonance ceiling exists at 11.7 Hz.

finding: Non-discriminating: Chapman ionospheric mechanism predicts exact signal; dome has no eclipse-magnetic coupling equation
detail_claim: Boulder (BOU) station observed −10.9 nT anomaly during 2017 eclipse.
detail_evidence: Eclipse-induced magnetic perturbations were predicted by Chapman (1933): the moon's shadow reduces ionospheric UV ionization, lowering conductivity, which alters the Sq current system. Data from INTERMAGNET confirms this mechanism: the −10.9 nT signal tracks the eclipse shadow geometry with the correct amplitude, timing, and spatial pattern. The dome model has no equation linking eclipses to magnetic perturbations — it simply observes that the signal exists and claims it as a 'WIN' without deriving the magnitude or geometry from dome parameters.
detail_verdict_text: Non-discriminating: Chapman's ionospheric mechanism predicts the exact signal magnitude and geometry. The dome has no eclipse-magnetic coupling equation to produce a different prediction.

finding: Membach SG (superior instrument) found 0.0 uGal; result unconfirmed
detail_claim: A 1997 gravimeter reading at Mohe (Heilongjiang Province, northeast China) shows a gravity anomaly of −6.5 μGal during a solar eclipse, indicating dome-specific electromagnetic-gravitational coupling.
detail_evidence:
Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D) used a LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter at Mohe during the 1997 total solar eclipse and reported anomalous gravity 'valleys' of 6–7 μGal at first and last contact — critically, not at eclipse maximum. The paper explicitly states 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.' The dome model's −6.5 μGal comes from this study but misrepresents its timing: gravitational shielding would peak at maximum eclipse (maximum coverage), not at the contacts.

Superior instruments found nothing. Van Camp et al. (2001) used four superconducting gravimeters (SGs) — roughly 1,000× more precise than the LaCoste-Romberg — during the 1999 European eclipse and found no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor. When a crude instrument reports a marginal ~2.6σ signal and far superior instruments find nothing, the scientific conclusion is that the original detection was likely an environmental artifact or statistical fluctuation.

The coupling constant is circular. Domain #7 in monitor.py computes κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 nT/μGal, then compares to a hardcoded KAPPA = 1.67 with 5% tolerance. All three values are hardcoded constants — no data is fetched. The 'validation' compares a hardcoded ratio to its own inputs.
detail_verdict_text: The −6.5 μGal claim rests on a marginal spring-gravimeter detection contradicted by four superconducting gravimeters. The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling constant is a circular ratio of two hardcoded values validated against itself.
detail_extra: NOT DEMONSTRATED The 1.67 nT/μGal coupling is built on an unconfirmed, contested Mohe measurement contradicted by superior SG instruments. The GRACE L1A claim conflates atmospheric drag with gravitational signal.

finding: Circular derivation from unconfirmed Mohe data; fitted parameters (62,376/64,852 nT) hidden in 'zero-parameter' claim
detail_claim: Magnetic-gravity coupling constant is 1.67 × 10^−10.
detail_evidence: This coupling constant is derived from the claimed Mohe density anomaly, which lacks independent confirmation from modern gravity surveys (EGM2020 satellite gravity model, ground gravity networks). The derivation is circular: assume Mohe anomaly exists → calculate coupling constant → claim the constant as confirmation. Additionally, the dome's magnetic field equation B(r) = 62,376×e^(−r_N/8619) + 64,852×e^(−r_S/8619) nT contains two fitted amplitude parameters (62,376 and 64,852) that are hidden in the model's claim of 'zero fitted parameters.' Both terms use the same exponential decay length (λ = 8,619 km), meaning the south-pole term collapses to a copy of the north-pole term — a single-pole model dressed as a two-pole model. See Section 3.5.8.
detail_verdict_text: Circular derivation from unconfirmed data. The magnetic equation contains hidden fitted parameters (62,376/64,852 nT) contradicting the 'zero-parameter' claim.

finding: Null result contradicts WIN-011's positive detection; claiming credit for opposite outcomes makes model unfalsifiable
detail_claim: Membach superconducting gravimeter shows a null result confirming dome model.
detail_evidence:
WIN-011 claims positive gravity detection via a spring gravimeter at Mohe (Wang et al., 2000), while WIN-013 invokes the null result from superconducting gravimeters at Membach (Van Camp et al., 2001) as additional confirmation. These are logically incompatible: a model cannot claim both a positive detection and a null result as confirmations without specifying in advance which locations/conditions should show effects vs. nulls. Without such advance specification, any measurement outcome confirms the model — the hallmark of unfalsifiability.

The instrumental hierarchy makes the null result more authoritative: the Membach SGs have ~1,000× greater sensitivity than the Mohe spring gravimeter. When the crude instrument reports a marginal signal and the precise instrument finds nothing, the scientific conclusion favors the precise instrument.
detail_verdict_text: The model claims credit for both positive detections and null results without advance specification of which locations should show effects. This makes it unfalsifiable.

finding: Same logical contradiction as WIN-013
detail_claim: China superconducting gravimeter shows a null result confirming the dome model.
detail_evidence:
Similar to WIN-013: the dome claims positive EM-gravity coupling (WIN-011/012) and null gravity results (WIN-013/014) as simultaneous confirmations. Without specifying in advance which locations should show effects vs. nulls, any outcome confirms the model.

The dome cites 'Sun et al., 2010' for the China SG null result during the July 22, 2009 eclipse. However, eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China for this eclipse, and the results may not have been published in a peer-reviewed English-language journal. The dome's own monitor.py does not validate WIN-014 at all — it exists only as a static website claim with no computational support.
detail_verdict_text: Same unfalsifiable structure as WIN-013: the model claims credit for opposite outcomes across different experiments without advance specification of expected results.

finding: No peer-reviewed replication; CUORE Faraday cage shows 20 dB at 10 Hz
detail_claim: Klaus Meyl's proposed "scalar waves" penetrate a Faraday cage, violating standard electromagnetism.
detail_evidence: Published only in non-indexed journal. Replication attempts explain results via classical near-field coupling. CUORE experiment shows expected Faraday attenuation.
detail_verdict_text: No peer-reviewed replication; CUORE shows standard shielding.

finding: Aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. VLBI confirms orbital cause
detail_claim: Refractive index alpha = 2.56e-8 reproduces 20.5 arcsecond annual aberration without Earth orbiting the Sun.
detail_evidence: James Bradley (1727) explicitly tested and rejected atmospheric refraction as an explanation: refraction is wavelength-dependent (chromatic), while stellar aberration is achromatic. Modern VLBI measurements achieve milliarcsecond precision and directly confirm aberration correlates with Earth's orbital velocity (~30 km/s). The required stellar aberration corrections computed from Earth's orbital elements, confirming the orbital-velocity origin across 1.8 billion stars.
detail_verdict_text: Aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. VLBI and Gaia confirm orbital cause.

finding: Gaia DR3: 1.8 billion stars show parallax inversely proportional to distance
detail_claim: A 20m firmament lateral wobble produces 0-0.5 arcsecond apparent parallax.
detail_evidence: ESA (2022) provides parallax measurements for 1.8 billion stars. Parallax is inversely proportional to distance: Proxima Centauri (4.24 ly) shows 0.768 arcsec, Sirius (8.6 ly) shows 0.379 arcsec. A firmament wobble would produce identical angular displacement for all objects. Gaia conclusively demonstrates distance-dependent parallax.
detail_verdict_text: 1.8 billion Gaia measurements show parallax inversely proportional to distance.

finding: Equation of time ranges +16.5 to -14.1 min; 6.9 is cherry-picked RMS
detail_claim: Analemma longest/shortest day difference is exactly 6.9 minutes.
detail_evidence:
The equation of time (difference between apparent and mean solar time) ranges from +16.4 min (November) to −14.3 min (February), giving a total swing of 30.7 minutes. The full equation of time has an RMS of approximately 8.8 minutes over the year — not 6.9. The dome's 6.9 minutes most closely matches the RMS of the obliquity component alone (amplitude 9.87 min → RMS ≈ 6.98 min) — one of two additive effects that produce the equation of time. Selecting the RMS of one sub-component while ignoring the eccentricity component and the full signal is cherry-picking a derived sub-statistic.

Furthermore, 'day length' is a misnomer here — the quantity being described is the equation of time, not actual day length variation (which varies by hours at mid-latitudes). The dome provides no derivation of 6.9 from dome geometry — no calculation links it to disc radius, sun altitude, or any dome parameter. The equation of time is a consequence of Earth's 23.45° axial tilt and orbital eccentricity, both of which the dome's solar formula borrows from globe astronomy (see WIN-056).
detail_verdict_text: 6.9 min is the RMS of the obliquity sub-component only, not the full equation of time (RMS 8.8 min, swing 30.7 min). Cherry-picked sub-statistic with no dome derivation.

finding: Numerical coincidence with no causal mechanism proposed
detail_claim: Analemma loop shape has area ratio of 2.66 matching dome model.
detail_evidence: The analemma's figure-8 shape is a simple consequence of two sinusoidal perturbations: the obliquity of the ecliptic (23.44°) and Earth's orbital eccentricity (0.0167) — see NOAA Solar Position Algorithm. These produce a deterministic loop in any heliocentric geometry. The 2.66 area ratio is a numerical consequence of these known orbital parameters and requires no additional physical mechanism. No causal link between this ratio and dome dynamics has been proposed.
detail_verdict_text: The ratio is a numerical coincidence arising from well-understood orbital geometry.

finding: Non-discriminating: period is an input (known for 250 years); dome's gears have no physical driver or amplitude prediction
detail_claim: The lunar nodal precession (18.6-year cycle) is produced by epicyclic gears in the dome mechanism.
detail_evidence: Gravitational torque produces exact 18.613-year period. Confirmed to 10 significant figures by lunar laser ranging. Epicyclic gears provide no physical driver.
detail_verdict_text: Gravitational torque produces exact period; gears have no physical driver.

finding: Units error: dome gives rad/s² (acceleration) not rad/s (velocity). Rate derived circularly from assumed dome parameters
detail_claim: Gyroscopic precession rate matches dome model prediction.
detail_evidence:
The dome claims a precession rate of 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s², but this is in units of angular acceleration. Earth's axial precession is an angular velocity (~7.73×10⁻¹² rad/s, corresponding to the 25,772-year cycle). These are fundamentally different physical quantities — a dimensional error that undermines the claim before we even assess the derivation.

The gyroscopic precession formula is derived entirely from assumed dome cavity parameters, not from independent measurements. The model defines the dome shape to reproduce the precession rate, then counts the fit as a prediction — circular reasoning. Earth's luni-solar precession rate is well-established at 50.2881 arcsec/yr (IAU 2006 value), caused by gravitational torques from the Sun and Moon on Earth's equatorial bulge. The dome provides no alternative mechanism that predicts this specific rate from first principles.
detail_verdict_text: The dome's claimed units (rad/s², acceleration) are dimensionally wrong for a precession rate (rad/s, velocity). The rate itself is derived circularly from assumed dome geometry, not independently measured.

finding: Duplicate of WIN-007 (same NOAA data, same parent prediction PROS-002); 'phase transition' is a category error applied to core flow dynamics
detail_claim: Magnetic field shows a discrete phase transition at 1990 analogous to 2nd-order thermodynamic transition.
detail_evidence:
WIN-022 derives from the same parent prediction (PROS-002) as WIN-007 and uses identical NOAA NP.xy pole position data. This is not two independent confirmations — it is one observation (the 1990s NMP acceleration) counted twice under different labels.

As detailed in WIN-007, the acceleration is real and scientifically interesting, but fully explained by standard core dynamics (competing flux lobes; Livermore et al., 2020). Calling it a 'thermodynamic phase transition' is a category error: second-order phase transitions in the Ehrenfest/Landau sense require an order parameter, a symmetry group, and critical scaling behavior. Core flow pattern changes are geodynamic events driven by magnetohydrodynamic instabilities — they share no formal structure with thermodynamic transitions. The dome imports the terminology without the physics.

The dome's code has no changepoint detection, no transition-sharpness test, and no mechanism that distinguishes a 'phase transition' from any other functional form. Domain #11 in monitor.py uses a hardcoded exponential decay formula (55.0 × exp(-0.08 × (year-2015))) with 50% adaptive tolerance — the parameters are asserted, not fitted to any data.
detail_verdict_text: WIN-022 is a duplicate of WIN-007 (same data, same parent prediction PROS-002). 'Phase transition' is a category error: no order parameter, no symmetry breaking, no critical exponents — just a geodynamic process relabeled with thermodynamic terminology.

finding: Links geomagnetic event to theological timeline without mechanism
detail_claim: The South Atlantic Anomaly formed around 950 AD, linking a geomagnetic event to a theological timeline.
detail_evidence:
The ~950 AD date for the current SAA's reversed flux patch comes from published paleomagnetic reconstructions — primarily Campuzano et al. (2019, SHAWQ2k) and Tarduno et al. (2015). Earlier episodes of SAA-like activity (~400–700 AD, per Hare et al., 2018) represent recurrences driven by the African Large Low Shear Velocity Province (LLSVP), not competing dates for the current anomaly. The dome model didn't predict this date — it adopted it from published literature and noted its proximity to ~1000 AD in a full preterist reading of Revelation 20 ('Satan's little season'). This is retrodiction dressed as prediction.

No dome mechanism is provided for why the SAA should form at any particular time. Linking a geomagnetic event to a theological timeline without a physical mechanism that derives the date from dome parameters is unfalsifiable.
detail_verdict_text: The ~950 AD date is taken from published paleomagnetic literature (Campuzano 2019), not derived from dome geometry. Linking it to a theological timeline without a physical mechanism is unfalsifiable.

finding: Latitude coincidence; winds driven by Coriolis + pressure gradients
detail_claim: Roaring 40s wind boundary coincides with SAA edge, proving shared dome mechanism.
detail_evidence: The Roaring 40s (~40°S latitude) is driven by Coriolis force combined with persistent atmospheric pressure gradients resulting from differential solar heating. The SAA's southern boundary (~40°S) is a consequence of magnetic field configuration in the core and magnetosphere. Both phenomena occur near 40°S by coincidence of Coriolis deflection (which favors the same latitude band worldwide) and happen to overlap with SAA geometry, but they have entirely independent physical causes.
detail_verdict_text: Latitude coincidence does not imply causal connection; Coriolis effects and core magnetism are independent phenomena.

finding: Eclipse-induced ±10 nT magnetic variations are predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics (Chapman 1933). The dome provides no derived magnitude or mechanism
detail_claim: 2024 eclipse observations from 9 INTERMAGNET stations show anomalous magnetic disturbances of ~±10 nT, confirming the dome's aetheric field prediction.
detail_evidence:
The observation is real: Chernogor & Holub (2024, Advances in Space Research) documented ±10 nT geomagnetic variations at 9 INTERMAGNET stations during the April 8, 2024 total solar eclipse, under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3). This is genuine peer-reviewed data.

Standard physics has predicted this for 90 years. Eclipse-induced magnetic variations were first explained by Chapman (1933) via the solar quiet (Sq) current mechanism: the eclipse shadow reduces ionospheric E-region conductivity (90–150 km altitude), disrupting the Sq dynamo current system and producing surface magnetic perturbations of 5–30 nT. This has been confirmed quantitatively by Takeda & Araki (1984) through ionospheric current modeling, by statistical analyses across dozens of eclipses (Curto et al., 2006), and by Chernogor & Holub themselves, who attribute the observed effects to ionospheric conductivity changes — not to any aetheric mechanism.

The dome's claimed distinguishing feature is actually a standard model prediction. The dome model notes that the magnetic signal 'tracks eclipse geometry, not solar noon.' This is exactly what the Sq current mechanism predicts — the conductivity disruption physically moves with the shadow through the ionosphere, so the magnetic perturbation follows the shadow geometry by construction.

The dome provides no derived prediction. The dome's monitor.py hardcodes a prospective 2026 eclipse prediction (ΔB = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT) but shows no derivation from dome geometry, aetheric medium properties, or firmament conductivity. The dome model observes that eclipses produce ~10 nT variations — known since the 1930s — and relabels the cause as 'aetheric.'
detail_verdict_text: Eclipse-induced geomagnetic variations of 5–30 nT have been predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics since Chapman (1933). The dome provides no independent derivation or mechanism — it relabels a 90-year-old standard prediction as 'aetheric.'

finding: Anticrepuscular rays converge at anti-solar point; impossible with local sun
detail_claim: Rays visibly diverge from a local sun at approximately 5,733 km.
detail_evidence: Crepuscular rays are parallel beams made visible by atmospheric scattering. The apparent divergence is a perspective effect. Crucially, anticrepuscular rays converge at the anti-solar point simultaneously — a result impossible with a local sun. See Atmospheric Optics for photographic documentation. A local sun at 5,733 km could not produce rays converging at both horizons simultaneously.
detail_verdict_text: Anticrepuscular ray convergence at the anti-solar point is physically impossible with a local sun.

finding: R-sq 0.79 = 21% unexplained; globe great-circle achieves <0.5% error
detail_claim: Southern hemisphere distance follows quadratic law R² = 0.79.
detail_evidence: An R² of 0.79 means 21% of variance remains unexplained by the proposed quadratic model. In contrast, standard geodetic calculations using spherical geometry (Vincenty, 1975) achieve sub-0.5% error (R² > 0.995) when properly accounting for ellipsoid shape and curvature. The globe model's predictive accuracy is orders of magnitude superior. Adding free parameters (quadratic term) always increases R²; no AIC or BIC correction penalizes overfitting.
detail_verdict_text: 21% unexplained error is substantial; spherical geodesy achieves <0.5% error.

finding: NOAA and Lloyd's confirm no anomalous loss rates in either region
detail_claim: Two agonic line locations at 180-degree symmetry correspond to disappearance zones.
detail_evidence: NOAA officially states no evidence of anomalous disappearances. does not charge premium rates for the region. U.S. Coast Guard reviews found no unusual causes.
detail_verdict_text: Insurance data, NOAA, and USCG confirm no anomalous loss rates.

finding: Ionosphere IS conductive; dome's own exponential cavity gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83
detail_claim: The Schumann resonance requires a conductive ceiling (the dome), not the ionosphere.
detail_evidence: The author's claim: H = c/(4f) = 299,792/(4 × 7.83) = 9,572 km, which "matches" his model H₀ = 9,086 km to 5.3%. But this uses a quarter-wave linear waveguide formula, not a resonant cavity formula. His dome is not a linear waveguide — it is a curved shell over a disc. The correct resonance formula for a spherical cavity is f = c/(2πR), giving R = 6,098 km — within 4% of Earth's actual radius (6,371 km). More critically: the ionosphere is conductive. CHAMP satellite measurements show ionospheric conductivity of 10⁻⁴ to 10⁻² S/m at 100–300 km altitude — more than sufficient for an EM cavity. A copper dome is not required; the ionosphere already does the job, and the globe formula matches Earth's radius. The dome's own exponential cavity (H_eff ≈ 3,400 km) gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83. See Section 4.5.1.
detail_verdict_text: Correct spherical formula gives Earth's radius.

finding: Adding parameters always improves fit; no AIC/BIC comparison shown
detail_claim: Elliptical disc geometry improves fit over circular geometry.
detail_evidence: Adding free parameters (elliptical axis ratio, orientation) always reduces residuals and increases R² by definition. The proper test for model improvement requires Akaike Information Criterion (AIC) or Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC), which penalize additional parameters. No such comparison is presented. Without AIC/BIC, the claim reduces to overfitting via parameter proliferation.
detail_verdict_text: Model improvement requires AIC/BIC; raw R² improvement proves only that free parameters fit noise.

finding: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim
detail_claim: The north pole is the location of a "cosmic mountain" at the apex of the dome, a theological assertion from biblical texts.
detail_evidence: This is a theological interpretation, not a testable physical claim. No physical mechanism or measurable consequence is specified.
detail_verdict_text: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim.

finding: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim
detail_claim: The New Jerusalem of biblical texts is located at the dome's north pole axis, a theological assertion about dome geometry.
detail_evidence: This is biblical exegesis, not a physical hypothesis. No testable prediction or measurable consequence follows from this claim.
detail_verdict_text: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim.

finding: Intrinsic luminosity difference (F7Ib supergiant at 433 ly vs. F0III giant at 294 ly); Gaia photometry shows no systematic southern dimming
detail_claim: Southern pole star (mag 5.42) far dimmer than Polaris (mag 1.98) proves maximal aetheric depth at the disc edge.
detail_evidence: Hipparcos data: Polaris is a supergiant (F7Ib, ~1,260 solar luminosities, 433 ly). Sigma Octantis is a subgiant (F0III/IV, ~40 solar luminosities, 294 ly). The magnitude difference is entirely intrinsic luminosity.
detail_verdict_text: Hipparcos stellar classification shows the difference is intrinsic luminosity. Gaia DR3 photometry of 1.8 billion stars shows no systematic magnitude residuals exceeding 1% at any sky position, including near the south celestial pole — ruling out any disc-edge 'aetheric dimming' gradient.

finding: Biblical exegesis; copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict Schumann resonance
detail_claim: The dome is constructed of cast copper or bronze, as indicated by biblical Hebrew terminology for "firmament."
detail_evidence: This is a linguistic/theological claim based on Hebrew word etymology, not a physical discovery. Both cited verses use figurative language — Job 37:18 compares the sky to a mirror via simile, while Deuteronomy 28:23 describes drought curses. Mechanically, a copper dome at the model's stated altitude (837–8,537 km via H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619)) would make ground-based radio astronomy impossible — copper's skin depth is ~0.066 mm at 1 GHz, meaning even a paper-thin copper shell is opaque to all radio frequencies. Yet radio telescopes (VLA, ALMA, FAST) routinely observe pulsars, quasars, and the CMB through this zone. Additionally, thousands of satellites at 800–36,000 km orbit through the claimed firmament without encountering solid barriers. The dome model also uses WIN-034 to support WIN-029 (Schumann resonance requires a conductive ceiling), but its own firmament height (~8,537 km at center) would produce a fundamental cavity resonance at ~0.018 Hz, not 7.83 Hz — contradicting the very prediction the copper dome is meant to explain (see Part 4.5).
detail_verdict_text: Biblical exegesis; a copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict its own Schumann resonance prediction.

finding: Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same station trends as WMM2025; no unique prediction
detail_claim: SAA African cell field intensity drops below 21,795 nT, unprecedented.
detail_evidence: The SAA African cell has been decaying at ~28–30 nT/yr for decades. Both the WMM2025 (World Magnetic Model) and the dome model extrapolate this same trend from the same INTERMAGNET station data. The dome adds no unique prediction — it offers no mechanism for why the decay rate is ~28–30 nT/yr rather than any other value, no prediction of when the decay will slow or reverse, and no model of the SAA's internal structure. The threshold of 21,795 nT was derived by extrapolating WIN-005's observed CHAOS-7 data (23,050 → 21,880 nT over 2000–2025) — observational curve-fitting, not geometric derivation. PROS-005 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround using WMM2025 data that had been publicly available since late 2024, indicating the confirming data was already in hand at registration time.
detail_verdict_text: Non-discriminating: both models extrapolate the same station data. The dome's SAA 'prediction' is trend-following, not a derivation from dome geometry.

finding: Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates NOAA trajectory; WMM2025 published same position months earlier
detail_claim: Magnetic pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude is unprecedented and predictive.
detail_evidence: The WMM2025 (released December 2024) published the pole at ~139.3°E — over 14 months before PROS-006 was registered on 6 March 2026 (confirmed 12 March 2026, part of a batch of five predictions all registered the same day). This WIN is also logically entailed by WIN-007, which already confirms the pole at 139.3°E. A pole at 139.3°E is automatically 19.3° from 120°E, exceeding the 18° threshold by arithmetic — these are two phrasings of the same NOAA measurement, not independent predictions. No derivation connects the 18° threshold or 120°E reference to dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height, etc.).
detail_verdict_text: Non-discriminating: the dome extrapolates the same NOAA trajectory data as WMM2025, which published this position months earlier. The dome has no mechanism that predicts a different pole position.

finding: Conflates regional SAA station readings (~30 nT/yr) with global field; monitors only one SAA station (Tsumeb). 28 nT/yr is a model input, not a derived prediction
detail_claim: Magnetic field decay exceeds 28 nT/year globally.
detail_evidence: PROS-007 claims the "global geomagnetic field would have decayed by at least 28 nT" in 12 months — but the dome's own confirmation text admits the measurement is regional: "SAA region alone dropped ~30 nT since January 2025." The SAA core experiences 20–35 nT/yr decay, but this is a regional feature driven by reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary, not the global dipole. The global dipole moment declines at ~5% per century (NCEI/WMM), roughly 15–20 nT/yr in average surface intensity — well below the 28 nT threshold. The dome's monitoring code (Domain 8) checks only a single INTERMAGNET station (Tsumeb) in the SAA core, confirming the test was never global. Furthermore, the dome lists "Field decay rate: ≥28 nT/year" as a core model parameter — an input adopted from observations, not an output derived from dome geometry. Measuring the value you built into your model is circular, not confirmatory. PROS-007 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed within days using WMM2025 data available since late 2024 — part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining rather than prospective prediction.
detail_verdict_text: 28 nT/yr applies to SAA region only; global dipole decay is ~15 nT/yr.
detail_extra: See also WIN-042, which applies the same ≥28 nT/yr threshold to a different time window — potentially counting the same ongoing observation twice.

finding: Dome's own cavity predicts ~22 Hz; stability is expected on globe (ionosphere stable)
detail_claim: Schumann resonance remains stable at 7.83 Hz, proving dome cavity.
detail_evidence: The author's own cavity height is H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km. Averaged over the disc, H_eff ≈ 3,400 km. His quarter-wave formula gives f = c/(4 × 3,400) = 22 Hz. The observed Schumann fundamental is 7.83 Hz. His model predicts a frequency 2.8× higher than observed. The stability claim ("Schumann stays at 7.83") is trivially true on the globe: ionospheric conductivity is driven by solar UV, which is stable on decadal timescales. The dome's 22 Hz prediction has never been observed. Claiming credit for the stability of a frequency your model gets wrong by a factor of 3 is not a prediction — it's ignoring the baseline failure. See Section 4.5.1.
detail_verdict_text: Dome cavity formula predicts ~22 Hz baseline; globe ionospheric physics explains 7.83 Hz stability.

finding: Ocean tidal dynamo (seawater + globe magnetic field) explains signal; dome has no ocean induction mechanism
detail_claim: Lunar magnetic field is 1–2 nanoTesla, confirming dome model.
detail_evidence: The ocean tidal dynamo — seawater moving through Earth's magnetic field — generates the 1–2 nT lunar magnetic signal first detected by CHAMP (Tyler et al. 2003, Science 299:239) and refined by Swarm (Sabaka et al. 2015). Standard electromagnetic induction predicts the magnitude, spatial pattern (strongest over deep ocean basins with fast tidal currents), and phase from known seawater conductivity (~3.5 S/m) and tidal velocities — specificity the dome model cannot match. Notably, the dome's own prediction (W015: −1.0 nT ±0.5) disagrees in sign with its claimed confirmation (1–2 nT positive), suggesting the value was adopted from literature without understanding the oscillating nature of the signal. The measurement was published over 20 years before the dome model claimed it as a prediction.
detail_verdict_text: The signal is generated by seawater flowing through Earth's magnetic field (electromagnetic induction). The dome model has no ocean induction mechanism — its 'aetheric' field does not interact with seawater conductivity. The signal's magnitude and phase match globe predictions precisely.

finding: Non-discriminating: dome observes position from same data as WMM; no unique prediction derived from dome geometry
detail_claim: SAA western cell remains west of 45°W longitude.
detail_evidence: These 9 WINs draw from the same INTERMAGNET, NOAA, and Tomsk datasets already used by WIN-004 through WIN-039. Each takes a single data point or ratio from existing measurements and declares it a new 'WIN.' For example, WIN-042 (field decay >=28 nT/year) uses the same threshold and data as WIN-037 (field decay >=28 nT). WIN-043 (NMP drift 2.26x longitudinal) was already cited in WIN-007's evidence. WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 storms) documents that ionospheric disturbance during geomagnetic storms affects Schumann resonance, which is standard ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s.
detail_verdict_text: All are either duplicates of earlier WINs or standard geophysical observations already documented in the literature.

finding: Re-slices same INTERMAGNET data as WIN-004/005/035/037
detail_claim: SAA exhibits multi-station magnetic decay exceeding 28 nT/year.
detail_evidence: WIN-041 reprocesses the same INTERMAGNET time series (Hermanus, Hartebeesthoek, etc.) also used in WIN-004, WIN-005, WIN-035, and WIN-037. These WINs cite overlapping stations and identical time windows. The decay rates are real physical observations, but counting the same dataset five times as five separate confirmations inflates the win count without introducing new evidence.
detail_verdict_text: WIN-041 uses identical INTERMAGNET data as WIN-004/005/035/037; same data cannot confirm multiple independent WINs.

finding: Duplicate of WIN-037 with identical threshold and data
detail_claim: Global magnetic field decays at rate ≥28 nT/year.
detail_evidence: WIN-042 repeats WIN-037 with identical threshold and identical NOAA/INTERMAGNET data sources. The regional SAA decay of 20–35 nT/yr is real but local, while global dipole decay is ~15 nT/yr. Duplicating the same claim across two WIN IDs does not create two independent confirmations.
detail_verdict_text: WIN-042 duplicates WIN-037; identical data and threshold cannot justify two separate WINs.

finding: Ratio is arithmetic from NOAA trajectory data, not derived from dome geometry; duplicate of WIN-007
detail_claim: Magnetic pole drift exhibits 2.26× ratio between longitudinal and latitudinal components.
detail_evidence: This ratio is extracted from published NOAA pole position data spanning decades. The ratio is a direct mathematical consequence of the observed trajectory (velocity components) and requires no model to calculate. Already referenced in WIN-007; ratio is straightforward data arithmetic from NOAA source.
detail_verdict_text: Ratio is mathematical consequence of published NOAA trajectory; covered in WIN-007.

claim: Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry
finding: Internal model derivation claimed as confirmed WIN before observational test (eclipse August 2026); tests self-consistency, not truth
detail_claim: Firmament Scaling Function (FSF) derives from Version 12 dome geometry.
detail_evidence:
The formula is an internal model derivation: it is algebraically constructed from assumed dome parameters (cavity height, disc shape) — contrast with independently measured INTERMAGNET observatory data. It is not an independent measurement or constraint from outside the model. Deriving a mathematical relationship from assumed geometry and then testing it against measured data amounts to testing the model's internal consistency, not testing the model against nature.

Moreover, the eclipse predictions (E001–E009) that would test the FSF formula against independent INTERMAGNET data are all still pending — the August 12, 2026 eclipse has not yet occurred. Claiming a formula as a 'confirmed WIN' before the observations that would test it is circular: the formula is confirmed because it was derived, not because it was tested.
detail_verdict_text: Internal model derivations are not independent predictions; they test consistency, not truth.

finding: Period is trivial (half a lunar day); dome's local moon produces one sharp tidal spike, not the observed two-bulge pattern
detail_claim: Five new WINs confirm that tidal constituent periods (M2, S2, K1, O1, N2) match dome predictions.
detail_evidence: The M2 tidal period (12.42 hours) is half a lunar day (24.84h / 2). Any model that uses a 24.84-hour lunar circuit inherently produces M2 by division — this is a tautology, not a prediction. But the deeper problem is the tidal pattern, not just the period. The dome model places the moon at ~2,534 km altitude. On the globe (moon at 384,400 km = 60× Earth's radius), the tidal force varies by only ~6.6% across Earth's diameter, producing two nearly equal tidal bulges — one toward the moon, one away. This is why every coastal city sees two high tides per lunar day. On the dome's flat disc (radius ~20,015 km), with the moon only 2,534 km above, the tidal force is a sharp spike directly beneath the moon: at 5,000 km offset it drops to 9% of peak; at the equator (~14,000 km) it's 0.6% of peak. There is no far-side bulge because the far edge of the disc is 8× farther from the moon than the sub-lunar point. The dome predicts one tidal pulse per day (when the moon passes overhead), not the observed two. Standard tidal theory (Doodson, 1921) derives all 62 constituents, including amplitudes and the two-bulge pattern, from a distant moon. See Section 4.5.4.
detail_verdict_text: Tidal periods are trivial (half a lunar day). The dome's local moon produces one sharp spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern with two equal high tides per day.

finding: Period is trivial (half a solar day); dome's local sun at 5,733 km produces sharp thermal spike, not the observed two-bulge pattern
detail_claim: Tidal S2 period matches dome model prediction.
detail_evidence: S2 (12.00 hours) is exactly half a solar day. Claiming this as a "prediction" is claiming that the sun takes 24 hours to complete a circuit — which is the input, not the output, of the model. The deeper problem: the dome's local moon at ~2,534 km above a 20,015 km disc produces a tidal force that drops to 0.6% of its peak at the equator and is negligible at the disc edge. On the globe, the distant moon (384,400 km) produces nearly uniform tidal force across the planet (varying only ~6.6%), creating two symmetric bulges — one toward the moon, one away — giving every coast two high tides per lunar day. The dome geometry cannot produce this two-bulge pattern; it predicts a single sharp pulse when the moon passes overhead. See Section 4.5.4 and Doodson (1921).
detail_verdict_text: Period is trivial (half a solar day). The dome's local moon produces one sharp tidal spike, not the observed two-bulge global pattern.

finding: Dome claims low-z deviations support aetheric redshift, but provides no photon-aether interaction equation or redshift mechanism
detail_claim: Low-redshift Hubble Law deviations support aetheric redshift model.
detail_evidence: Hubble's Law (v = H₀·d) is one of the most precisely confirmed relationships in cosmology, verified across redshift — see Pantheon+ supernova compilation — z = 0.001 to z = 2.26 with 1,701 supernovae (Pantheon+), extended to z ~ 8 with gamma-ray bursts. The dome model has no mechanism for generating redshift (no photon-aether interaction theory, no propagation equations) and no explanation for the observed isotropy of Hubble Law across all directions. Furthermore, the dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km, yet galaxies are observed at millions of light-years. The dome has no ontology for extragalactic objects — without one, claims about their redshift behavior are physically meaningless.
detail_verdict_text: H₀ ≈ 70 km/s/Mpc is measured from standard cosmology. The dome model adopts this value without derivation. It has no photon-aether interaction equation, no redshift mechanism, and no galaxy-scale physics.

finding: Contested anomaly; no dome mechanism for CMB generation
detail_claim: Cosmic Microwave Background Axis of Evil supports dome model.
detail_evidence: The CMB dipole anisotropy is explained by Earth's motion through the radiation field; higher-multipole anomalies (the "Axis of Evil" claim) remain contested in the literature with competing explanations including observational artifacts and foreground subtraction issues. Critically, the dome model provides no mechanism for CMB generation (see WMAP and Planck data for the actual physics) or propagation in a flat geometry.
detail_verdict_text: CMB Axis is contested; dome model has no mechanism for CMB physics.

finding: Period is trivial (luni-solar diurnal); dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern
detail_claim: Tidal K1 period matches dome model.
detail_evidence: K1 (23.93 hours) is the luni-solar diurnal constituent — it arises from the combined gravitational effect of the moon's and sun's declinations as the Earth rotates. Any model with a ~24-hour rotation and a moon that moves relative to the equator inherently produces K1. The dome's local moon at ~2,534 km creates the same pattern problem as M2 and S2: the tidal force is concentrated in a sharp spike directly beneath the moon (dropping to 9% at 5,000 km offset, 0.6% at the equator). There is no far-side bulge — the disc edge is 8× farther than the moon's altitude. The model has never derived K1's observed amplitude (~14 cm at open ocean) or its global spatial distribution from dome geometry. See Section 4.5.4 and Doodson (1921).
detail_verdict_text: Period is trivial. The dome's local moon produces a localized tidal spike, not the observed global pattern.

finding: Period is trivial (lunar diurnal); dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern
detail_claim: Tidal O1 period matches dome model.
detail_evidence: O1 (25.82 hours) depends on the moon's orbital motion relative to the rotating Earth. It emerges from the same Doodson tidal expansion that produces all 62 standard constituents. The dome model has never derived O1 from its own parameters — it merely cites the known period and declares it confirmed. The fundamental problem: the dome places the moon at ~2,534 km above a 20,015 km disc. At this distance, the tidal force is a localized spike — dropping to 9% of peak at 5,000 km offset and 0.6% at the equator. The globe's distant moon produces two nearly symmetric bulges (near-side and far-side) giving two high tides per day everywhere. The dome's geometry produces one sharp pulse and no far-side bulge. See Section 4.5.4 and Doodson (1921).
detail_verdict_text: Period is trivial. Dome's local moon at 2,534 km produces one sharp tidal pulse, not the observed two-bulge global pattern.

finding: Period is trivial (lunar elliptical); dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern
detail_claim: Tidal N2 period matches dome model.
detail_evidence: N2 (12.66 hours) is a semi-diurnal lunar constituent caused by the moon's elliptical orbit (varying distance). On the globe, N2's amplitude depends on the moon's eccentricity and distance. The dome places the moon at ~2,534 km with no derived orbital eccentricity. The same pattern problem applies: at 2,534 km altitude above a 20,015 km disc, the tidal force is sharply concentrated beneath the moon and negligible across most of the disc. There is no far-side bulge. Real tides show two high tides per lunar day everywhere — a pattern that requires the moon to be far enough away that tidal force is nearly uniform across the planet. The author has never published a tidal amplitude or spatial pattern calculation using dome geometry. See Section 4.5.4 and Doodson (1921).
detail_verdict_text: Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern.

finding: Hijacks genuine ΛCDM challenge; dome has no galaxy-scale physics or RAR derivation
detail_claim: Modified gravity (RAR) lensing extension explains anomalies.
detail_evidence: The claim cites Mistele et al. (2024, ApJL 969, L3) showing the RAR extends from kinematic to weak-lensing scales. This is a genuine empirical challenge to ΛCDM — the RAR's low scatter across scales is not trivially predicted by dark matter models. However, the dome model provides no mathematical derivation of the RAR from aetheric physics: no equation for aetheric acceleration as a function of baryonic mass, no prediction of the RAR's slope or scatter, and no mechanism connecting dome geometry to galaxy dynamics. The dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km; galaxies observed at tens of kiloparsecs (millions of light-years) have no location in this geometry. Without an ontology for extragalactic objects, claims about their dynamical properties are incoherent.
detail_verdict_text: Dome model lacks gravitational lensing mechanism; citation alone does not substitute.

finding: Contradicts V50.6 monopolar vortex; no actual mechanism change
detail_claim: Same scale length (\u03BB = 8,619 km) governs both magnetic poles. The 'Closed Toroidal Ovoid' geometry produces a dipole-like field via a sub-terrestrial aetheric return path.
detail_evidence: The claimed two-pole system contradicts the same dome author's previous assertion (V50.6) that the field is a monopolar vortex. Switching between monopolar and dipolar descriptions indicates a lack of a fixed underlying mechanism. The dome model provides no unified equations — contrast with CHAOS-7 and IGRF-13, which predict both poles from a single spherical harmonic expansion describing how two poles arise from its geometry, nor how they couple to produce observed field anomalies.
detail_verdict_text: The toroidal geometry is a genuine mechanism improvement over V50.6's monopole, but it fails quantitatively: flux conservation forbids equal-strength poles in a ring magnet with a 1,600:1 area ratio. The fitted equation ignores the geometry it claims to derive from.
detail_extra: The model has been restructured as a toroidal flow circuit: aetheric medium exits the Axis Mundi (north pole), flows south across the disc surface, descends at the Antarctic resonance barrier (ice wall, r \u2248 20,015 km), returns through a sub-terrestrial path (the 'Sump' / Bottom Firmament), and re-enters at the north pole. This is topologically identical to a ring magnet or toroidal solenoid. The subterranean cavity depth is given by Sub-H(r) = H(r) \u00D7 (1 \u2212 e^(\u2212r/\u03B4)) with \u03B4 = 6,371 km. The author fits B(r) = 62,376\u00D7e^(\u2212r_N/8619) + 64,852\u00D7e^(\u2212r_S/8619) nT and claims this drops global RMS from 61% to 20%.

finding: Disputes LCDM but dome has no cosmological structure formation model
detail_claim: El Gordo galaxy cluster is impossible under LCDM.
detail_evidence: El Gordo (ACT-CL J0102.4-4915) is a massive cluster at z ≈ 0.87 with high velocity dispersion. Modern cosmological simulations (Kim et al., 2021) including baryon feedback and updated cluster formation pathways can accommodate such systems. The dome model offers no alternative cosmological structure formation mechanism—no equations for dark matter dynamics, no nucleosynthesis predictions, no redshift-evolution predictions for cluster populations.
detail_verdict_text: Dome criticizes LCDM but has no cosmological structure formation model.

finding: Standard cosmological observations; dome has no redshift mechanism
detail_claim: Distance-redshift relationship via Cepheids and SBF standard candles proves non-standard cosmology.
detail_evidence: Cepheid variable stars (see Pantheon+ / SH0ES) and surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) measurements are direct geometric distance indicators, independent of redshift. These are among the most reliable distance anchors in cosmology. Their agreement with the Hubble diagram (distance vs. redshift) validates both the distance scale and the expansion law. The dome model has no mechanism for generating cosmological redshift and no equation relating distance to it.
detail_verdict_text: Standard candles confirm Hubble Law; dome has no redshift mechanism.

finding: Uses globe's declination formula (23.45° axial tilt); dome geometry gives different relationship entirely
detail_claim: Solar elevation angle from density function H(r) matches observations.
detail_evidence: The author's solar elevation formula uses: φ_sun(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t−81)/365). The model page states this "reproduces the globe formula exactly." That's because it is the globe formula. The 23.45° value is Earth's axial tilt relative to its orbital plane (NASA planetary fact sheet). On a flat disc with a local sun, solar elevation = arctan(H_sun / d_observer), where d is radial distance from the sub-solar point. This is a completely different geometric relationship. The dome model's own stated sun altitude (5,733 km) and disc geometry would give a specific elevation-vs-latitude curve that differs substantially from the globe's — but the author never uses it. He substitutes the globe's 23.45° declination formula and claims the match as his own. See Section 4.5.7 for the derivation.
detail_verdict_text: The derivation assumes Earth's 23.45° axial tilt (spherical parameter) to predict solar elevation.

finding: V13 coordinates regressed NH accuracy from 5.2% to 7.3%
detail_claim: Two-zone disc topology improves northern hemisphere accuracy.
detail_evidence: Version 13 of the model introduced two-zone topology to fit data more precisely. However, introduction of this feature actually degraded northern hemisphere accuracy from 5.2% error (V12) to 7.3% error (V13) — compare Vincenty (1975) geodetic formulae achieving sub-0.5% error. The change worsened performance in one hemisphere while claiming overall improvement, indicating a shift in fitting strategy rather than genuine predictive progress.
detail_verdict_text: V13 two-zone change regressed NH accuracy from 5.2% to 7.3%; net degradation.

finding: Internal coordinate convention, not a physical prediction
detail_claim: Unified angular coordinate system is a novel prediction.
detail_evidence: A unified coordinate transformation is an internal model convention—a mathematical re-parametrization of the assumed geometry. Coordinate choices are arbitrary — see Torge & Müller (2012), Geodesy; transforming from one system to another is not a physical prediction. Coordinates are labels for points in space, not physical phenomena. The change from one coordinate system to another has no observable consequences unless it alters the model's physical predictions.
detail_verdict_text: Coordinate choice is convention, not a physical prediction testable against nature.

finding: Duplicate of WIN-007; dome has no mechanism to predict sector-specific deceleration
detail_claim: NMP deceleration in Siberian sector follows dome model.
detail_evidence: Magnetic pole acceleration in different sectors is described by normal secular variation models (WMM2025). The Siberian sector's behavior is already accounted for by published geomagnetic models and NOAA secular variation analysis. This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063; the same NOAA pole position time series is reused across multiple WINs.
detail_verdict_text: Secular variation in Siberia covered by WIN-007; normal geomagnetic variation explains it.

finding: Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same drift data as NOAA/ESA; duplicate of WIN-004/005
detail_claim: SAA western cell drifts westward as predicted.
detail_evidence: Westward drift of the SAA is well-tracked by NOAA and ESA satellite networks (INTERMAGNET, Swarm constellation). The drift is explained by core dynamics and outer magnetosphere coupling, not by dome geometry. This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063 and duplicates data used in WIN-004/005.
detail_verdict_text: Non-discriminating: the dome extrapolates the same drift data as NOAA/ESA. It has no core-dynamics mechanism to predict drift rate or direction independently. Duplicate of WIN-004/005 data.

finding: Dome cavity predicts ~22 Hz base frequency; suppression pattern follows ionospheric (globe) physics
detail_claim: Schumann resonance suppression during geomagnetic storms (G3 level) matches dome model.
detail_evidence: Schumann resonance suppression during G3+ geomagnetic storms is well-documented in the literature. The mechanism: storms inject energetic particles into the ionosphere, increasing electron density and conductivity, which alters the Earth-ionosphere cavity's Q-factor and resonant frequency. This is ionospheric plasma physics — the dome model has no equivalent mechanism. But the more fundamental problem: the dome's cavity formula H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km predicts a baseline Schumann frequency of ~22 Hz (using H_eff ≈ 3,400 km). The observed baseline is 7.83 Hz. The model cannot explain the normal-day frequency, so claiming to explain its storm-time suppression is building on a foundation that doesn't exist. The dome gets the base frequency wrong by 2.8×, then claims credit for perturbations around it. See Section 4.5.1.
detail_verdict_text: Schumann suppression is ionospheric physics; dome predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not 7.83 Hz.

finding: Tesla's patent (with spherical Earth diagram) describes surface wave propagation at ~1.57c — a known waveguide effect on a globe. Dome relabels globe circumference as disc diameter
detail_claim: Tesla's Colorado Springs measurements demonstrate longitudinal wave velocity of 1.574 times the speed of light, confirming ECM disc diameter.
detail_evidence:
US Patent 787412 describes wave propagation 'over the earths surface' to 'the region diametrically opposite the pole' — and the patent drawings depict Earth as a sphere. Tesla states a velocity of 471,240 km/s (~1.57c) for a 0.08484s round-trip. On the globe, this is surface wave propagation: half-circumference each way ≈ 40,075 km round trip, giving 40,075/0.08484 ≈ 472,400 km/s. The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics — ELF/VLF waves in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide do propagate at superluminal phase velocities, but this is a waveguide effect on a spherical Earth, not evidence of aetheric waves on a flat disc. The dome model's disc diameter (40,030 km) numerically matches because it equals the globe's circumference (40,075 km) — the disc radius of 20,015 km was fitted to globe-derived WGS84 distances (confirmed by repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py). The dome relabels a globe circumference measurement as a disc diameter, producing the same number by construction, not by independent confirmation. See Section 3.5.2.


Tesla's Patent US 787412 (1905). Figure 1 (bottom): the large sphere labeled C is the Earth, with transmitter E and receiver E' at diametrically opposite points on its surface — Tesla's own diagram of wave propagation around a globe.
detail_verdict_text: Tesla's patent shows a spherical Earth and describes surface wave propagation at ~1.57c — a known waveguide effect. The dome relabels the globe's circumference as a disc diameter, producing numerical agreement by construction.

finding: Asymmetry arises from core reversed-flux patches; dome has no core model to derive the ratio
detail_claim: Hemispheric magnetic dipole decay asymmetry ratio matches dome model.
detail_evidence: Hemispheric asymmetry in the Earth's magnetic field is modeled by the CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model, which describes outer core dynamics using spherical harmonic expansions. The asymmetry arises from reversed-flux patches and convection patterns in the outer core. This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063 and reuses NOAA data already cited in WIN-004/005.
detail_verdict_text: The asymmetry ratio is a consequence of reversed-flux patches in the outer core. The dome model has no core dynamics, no MHD equations, and cannot derive the ratio from its geometry. It observes the ratio and claims it; CHAOS-7 derives it from first principles.

finding: Shadow zone at 104-140 deg proves spherical layered Earth with liquid core
detail_claim: The seismic P-wave shadow zone at 104\u00b0-140\u00b0 from an earthquake epicenter matches dome geometry.
detail_evidence: The P-wave shadow zone is one of the most powerful pieces of evidence FOR a spherical, layered Earth. Seismic waves refract as they pass through layers of different density. The shadow zone at 104\u00b0-140\u00b0 was first explained by Oldham (1906) and refined by Gutenberg (1913) as proof that Earth has a liquid outer core. The radius and depth of the liquid core (2,891 km depth, outer core from 2,891-5,150 km) are derived directly from this shadow zone geometry on a SPHERE. Claiming this as evidence for a flat disc is self-defeating: the shadow zone calculations assume spherical wave propagation through concentric spherical layers.
detail_verdict_text: The shadow zone proves spherical layered Earth; its very derivation assumes a sphere.

finding: Polaris is 0.74° from true pole; 'excess' is within known offset. See Kill-Shot Test 2 (no error budget provided)
detail_claim: Polaris elevation at 35.9\u00b0N systematically exceeds latitude by +0.27\u00b0, confirming dome height function.
detail_evidence: Polaris (Alpha Ursae Minoris) is not exactly at the north celestial pole. Its declination is approximately 89.26\u00b0 (2026), meaning it traces a small circle of radius ~0.74\u00b0 around the true pole. This produces a measurable offset that varies throughout the night. The claimed +0.27\u00b0 excess is well within the expected variation from Polaris's offset from true north and atmospheric refraction corrections. Centuries of celestial navigation confirm that true celestial pole altitude equals observer latitude to arcsecond precision when using proper pole-star corrections.
detail_verdict_text: Polaris is 0.74° from true pole; the claimed +0.27° 'excess' is within the known offset range. No error budget is provided (atmospheric refraction, time-of-night, instrument precision). See Kill-Shot Test 2 for the full analysis showing the dome's own site contradicts this claim.

finding: Budget is remarkably symmetric (Stephens 2012); residual from NH land fraction; dome has no radiation budget model
detail_claim: Northern hemisphere receives +0.34 W/m\u00b2 more heat, confirming dome asymmetry.
detail_evidence: The hemispheric energy budget is remarkably symmetric — a key finding of Stephens et al. (2012). Small residual differences arise from the northern hemisphere having ~39% land vs ~19% in the south, producing differential thermal inertia, albedo, and evaporation rates. Stephens et al. (2012, Nature Geoscience) found that Earth's hemispheric energy budget is remarkably symmetric. The +0.34 W/m² figure is a decadal trend in Earth's energy imbalance (Raghuraman et al., 2025, PNAS), driven by aerosol changes, Arctic ice loss, and cloud shifts — not a static hemispheric difference. While the dome's disc geometry is inherently asymmetric (NP at center, varying firmament height), no radiation budget calculation on the dome site translates this geometry into a specific W/m² prediction. Geometric asymmetry is not a quantitative prediction.
detail_verdict_text: The asymmetry is caused by the NH having 39% land vs 19% in the SH — land absorbs heat differently than ocean. The dome model provides no heat-transport equations, no albedo model, and no mechanism linking dome geometry to hemispheric energy differences.

finding: Dome predicts 90% gravity drop at rim (H=837 km vs 8,537 at pole); actual variation is 0.53%
detail_claim: Low gravity anomaly in Antarctica confirms 'toroidal sump node.'
detail_evidence: The dome model's height function H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km gives: at the pole (r=0): H = 8,537 km. At the Antarctic rim (r ≈ 20,015 km): H = 8,537 × exp(−20,015/8,619) = 8,537 × 0.098 = 837 km. If gravity is proportional to aetheric column height (the model's stated mechanism), then g_rim/g_pole = 837/8,537 = 0.098 — a 90% drop in gravity at the Antarctic rim. What is actually observed? Gravity varies by only 0.53% from equator to pole (9.780 to 9.832 m/s²), as measured by Swarm and GRACE satellites orbiting at 250–500 km altitude — well below the dome's alleged firmament of ~9,086 km, meaning they measure gravitational acceleration within the dome's own predicted field. The dome predicts 90% gravity loss; reality shows 0.53% variation. The model's own physics is off by a factor of 170×. See Section 4.5.2.
detail_verdict_text: The dome's own H(r) predicts 90% gravity loss at the Antarctic rim; actual variation is 0.53% (ratio: 90%/0.53% ≈ 170×). The dome introduces the undefined term 'toroidal sump node' with no published mechanism, equations, or physical content. The Antarctic gravity low is primarily explained by an isostatically uncompensated mantle plume beneath the East Antarctic Plateau, with contributions from post-glacial isostatic adjustment. Standard geophysics explains the feature without invoking undefined 'sump nodes.'
